target.title: fix(wechat): preserve existing accounts across hot reload
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve known live accounts when a channel hot-reload restarts without an explicit account id
- treat hot-reload stop/restart as a manager-owned handoff so existing accounts restart instead of getting stuck in a manual-stop state
- document the WeChat multi-account behavior and cover the hot-reload path with regression tests
- add a guard that ordinary caller-owned restarts still do not resurrect stale accounts
- harden the non-isolated agent model-catalog visibility coverage that timed out in refreshed CI

## Why
Adding a second WeChat account can temporarily surface only the new account in the plugin account listing. Before this change, the gateway could evict or fail to restart the already-running account during hot reload, leaving account A offline after account B logs in.

## Testing
- `pnpm install`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts` (3 files, 64 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts` (1 file, 24 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 1 file, 3 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.moonshot.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.policy.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 5 files, 15 tests passed)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: plugin hot reload for `openclaw-weixin` treats the pre-replace channel stop as a manager-owned handoff and restarts the active channel with known accounts included.

Real environment tested: clean temporary OpenClaw contributor worktree on Rohin's Mac mini at commit `82f88803b6`; Node v25.9.0; pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: the gateway hot-reload regression suite passed with 3 test files and 64 tests, including the plugin pre-replace rollback and deferred channel reload paths that restart with `{ includeKnownAccounts: true }`, plus the ordinary restart guard that proves stale known accounts are not resurrected outside the hot-reload safety-net path.

Observed result after fix: the hot-reload path calls `stopChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { manual: false })` before plugin replacement, then `startChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { includeKnownAccounts: true })` after replacement, so the manager-owned reload path does not mark the account as manually stopped before account preservation runs.

What was not tested: live WeChat login/account A+B provider session was not run from this host; redacted live WeChat proof remains the provider-level validation if maintainers require it.

## AI assisted
- OpenAI Codex

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.

target.title: fix(gateway): clear admin scopes for backend self-pairing
target.body:
Summary
- Stop treating shared token/password auth as sufficient to preserve any self-declared operator scopes for local backend self-pairing.
- Keep unscoped backend local handoff intact, while routing scoped local backend Gateway calls through normal device identity so internal loopback calls still work.
- Add explicit issue coverage for loopback/no-Origin backend identity + shared token/password + self-declared operator scopes + no device identity.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Device-less local/shared-secret backend Gateway clients must not preserve self-declared operator scopes while skipping pairing.

**Real environment tested:** Temporary local OpenClaw Gateway from this PR branch, token auth, loopback WebSocket at `ws://127.0.0.1:39139`, channels skipped, configured via a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Started the local Gateway from this branch, then ran a Node/tsx WebSocket client using `GatewayClient` as `gateway-client` / `backend` with shared token auth, `deviceIdentity: null`, and `scopes: ["operator.write"]`. The client then attempted `sessions.list`.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the live local Gateway client:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr86192-proof-... \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr86192-proof-.../openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=39139 \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=secret \
  node --import tsx --input-type=module

local gateway: ws://127.0.0.1:39139
backend shared-token no-device requested scopes: [operator.write]
hello auth scopes: []
sessions.list error: missing scope: operator.read
```

Gateway runtime log excerpt for the same live connection:

```text
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ sessions.list 1ms errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=missing scope: operator.read
```

**Observed result after fix:** The backend shared-token, no-device connection completed, but the server returned no authorized scopes in `hello-ok`; the follow-up read-scoped RPC failed with `missing scope: operator.read`. This verifies that non-admin self-declared scopes are cleared too, not only `operator.admin`.

**What was not tested:** Remote browser-origin behavior was not live-tested here; focused regression coverage covers the local and shared-secret-loopback cases changed by this PR.

Additional verification
```text
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/handshake-auth-helpers.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.compat-baseline.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.test.ts

Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  165 passed (165)

$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)

$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  30 passed (30)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#72418

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add ClickClack slash command suggestions
target.body:
## Summary
- enrich the shared native command registry with usage/menu metadata for slash command autocomplete
- add a gateway-authenticated ClickClack plugin route at `POST /clickclack/commands/suggest` for composer slash command suggestions
- filter suggestions through ClickClack account allowlists plus `commands.allowFrom` and `commands.ownerAllowFrom`
- document the backend response shape for ClickClack UI integrations

## Real behavior proof
Setup: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/clickclack-slash-commands`, using a temporary OpenClaw home with dummy/redacted ClickClack config and a throwaway gateway on `127.0.0.1:18799`. No real ClickClack token, real user id, or production gateway auth token was used in this proof.

Temporary proof config used these redacted values:

```json
{
  "gateway": { "mode": "local", "auth": { "mode": "token", "token": "proof-token-redacted" } },
  "commands": {
    "allowFrom": { "clickclack": ["usr_local"] },
    "ownerAllowFrom": ["clickclack:usr_owner"]
  },
  "channels": {
    "clickclack": {
      "enabled": true,
      "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8080",
      "token": "ccb_proof_redacted",
      "workspace": "wsp_local",
      "defaultTo": "channel:general",
      "allowFrom": ["usr_local"],
      "replyMode": "agent"
    }
  }
}
```

Commands run after the patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr86203-proof HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr86203-proof \
  node dist/index.js gateway --port 18799

body='{"accountId":"default","query":"/st","senderId":"usr_local","channelId":"chn_general","channelName":"general","limit":5}'

curl -sS -o /tmp/pr86203-unauth.json -w '%{http_code}\n' \
  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18799/clickclack/commands/suggest \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data "$body"

curl -sS -o /tmp/pr86203-auth.json -w '%{http_code}\n' \
  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18799/clickclack/commands/suggest \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer proof-token-redacted' \
  --data "$body"

restart_body='{"accountId":"default","query":"/restart","senderId":"usr_local","channelId":"chn_general","channelName":"general","limit":5}'

curl -sS -o /tmp/pr86203-restart.json -w '%{http_code}\n' \
  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18799/clickclack/commands/suggest \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer proof-token-redacted' \
  --data "$restart_body"
```

Observed result:

```text
unauth_status=401
auth_status=200
auth_restart_status=200
```

Parsed response summary:

```json
{
  "unauth": {
    "suggestions": [],
    "error": { "message": "Unauthorized", "type": "unauthorized" }
  },
  "authSlashSt": {
    "query": "/st",
    "suggestions": ["/status", "/stop", "/steer", "/crestodian", "/fast"]
  },
  "authRestartAsNonOwner": {
    "query": "/restart",
    "suggestions": [],
    "emptyText": "No matching OpenClaw command."
  }
}
```

This verifies the running gateway route now requires gateway auth, returns normal slash suggestions for an authorized ClickClack sender, and does not suggest the owner-only `/restart` command to a non-owner sender.

Not tested yet: a browser-level ClickClack UI autocomplete flow. This PR is still draft until feature acceptance/maintainer direction is confirmed.

## Validation
- `pnpm test:extension clickclack` (5 files, 22 tests passed)
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` on changed files
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm build`

## Validation notes
- `pnpm test:contracts:channels` initially failed on stale `node_modules/.experimental-vitest-cache` temp-file ENOENT. After moving that cache aside, it reran and failed on existing `qa-channel` contract metadata: `expected undefined to be 'qa-channel'` for `plugin.meta.id`. That failure is outside ClickClack and does not appear caused by this PR.
- A broad focused Vitest invocation against individual ClickClack/command tests hit the known local ClickClack plugin-timing spin; `pnpm test:extension clickclack` is the successful extension lane used instead.
- `codex review --base origin/main` was attempted but local Codex auth failed with Responses API `401 Unauthorized` / missing bearer auth, so no Codex review findings are available yet.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This PR was prepared with Codex assistance. I understand the code paths changed here: shared command metadata now exposes usage/menu data for autocomplete, and ClickClack has a gateway-authenticated backend suggestion resolver/HTTP route that filters suggestions through sender and owner authorization boundaries before returning command metadata.

target.title: fix(agent): prevent sticky model fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- roll back temporary auto fallback model overrides after successful fallback attempts
- keep session routing pointed at the selected model while still recording usage/cost for the actual fallback model used
- update runner/session accounting coverage and add the Unreleased changelog entry

## Root cause
Successful fallback candidates were persisted into session override state but only rolled back on errors/classified failures. Usage persistence also wrote `providerUsed`/`modelUsed` into the session identity, so one fallback run could pin later turns to the smaller fallback model even after the primary model recovered.

## Validation
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A temporary model fallback should not make the session stick to the fallback model. After a primary-model rate limit sends one turn to a configured fallback, the next turn in the same session should try the configured primary again while usage/status still records the actual fallback runtime used for the fallback turn.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout at PR head `c4f5337a7fdcc9f11c17ff6f42d8e8295fb77eed`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/home`, isolated workspace `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/workspace`, and a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1`. No real user session, live channel, or production OpenClaw home was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configured `agents.defaults.model.primary` as `primary-local/primary-model` with `fallbacks: ["fallback-local/fallback-model"]`.
2. Started a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` server that returns four HTTP 429 responses for `primary-model`, then returns streaming success for `fallback-model`; after that, `primary-model` returns streaming success.
3. Ran two local embedded agent turns against the same session id:

`/usr/bin/node openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id pr75270-proof --thinking off --json --timeout 120 --message <proof message>`

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/proof-output.json`:

```json
{
  "first": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 fallback proof OK",
    "provider": "fallback-local",
    "model": "fallback-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [
      {
        "provider": "primary-local",
        "model": "primary-model",
        "error": "429 proof primary rate limit",
        "reason": "rate_limit",
        "status": 429
      }
    ],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "fallback-model",
      "fallback-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "fallback-local",
      "model": "fallback-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "second": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 primary recovered OK",
    "provider": "primary-local",
    "model": "primary-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "primary-local",
      "model": "primary-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "allRequestModels": [
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model"
  ]
}
```

The runtime log also showed the fallback decision on the first turn:

```text
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=primary-local/primary-model reason=rate_limit next=fallback-local/fallback-model detail=429 proof primary rate limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=fallback-local/fallback-model reason=unknown next=none
```

Observed result after fix: The first turn used the configured fallback after the synthetic primary returned 429, and `fallbackAttempts` recorded the primary failure. The same session's persisted auto override fields were clear after the fallback turn. The second turn, with the primary endpoint recovered, requested and completed on `primary-local/primary-model` with no fallback attempts. This verifies the session did not stay pinned to the fallback model.

What was not tested: This proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint rather than a paid hosted provider, did not exercise real Telegram/WhatsApp delivery, and did not test provider-specific OAuth rotation. Those paths are covered separately by the automated tests listed above.

target.title: fix(auth): auto-migrate keychain-only legacy Codex OAuth profiles on first interactive CLI run
target.body:
## Summary

Closes the remaining bucket from #85083: macOS users whose legacy `oauthRef` sidecar seed lives only in the Keychain still had to know to run `openclaw doctor --fix` after upgrade, because the embedded-runtime self-heal that landed in #85074 intentionally runs with `allowKeychainPrompt: false`.

The fix adds a tiny pre-command trigger in `runCli` (`src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts`) that, on darwin + TTY only, reuses the existing `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` doctor flow when a sidecar dir is present. Fast bail-outs make the cost a single `readdirSync` for users without legacy sidecars. Declining writes a permanent decline marker in the state dir (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/legacy-oauth-sidecar-migration-declined`) so we never pester the user again; running `openclaw doctor --fix` is the documented retry path. `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR=0` is an explicit opt-out for power users.

Headless paths (cron, systemd, Telegram polling, embedded sub-agent dispatch) still skip the prompt — but the legacy sidecar loader now emits a one-shot `log.warn` that names `openclaw doctor --fix` and macOS Keychain, instead of letting the credential silently fall through to a downstream `No API key found for provider "openai-codex"`.

Also drops the user-confusing `sidecar-backed` phrasing from the doctor prompt/notes/changes; existing internal identifier names are left intact.

## Approach

Approach **B** from the issue (auto-trigger doctor migration on first interactive `openclaw <anything>` invocation). Architecturally cleaner than threading TTY-awareness into the embedded runtime: the trigger reuses doctor's existing migrate function as-is, and "when is it safe to prompt for Keychain access?" stays one decision, one place.

Skip rules in order: `process.platform !== "darwin"` → `!isInteractiveTty` → `CI=true || OPENCLAW_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 || OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR=0` → `OPENCLAW_AUTH_STORE_READONLY=1` → `--help/--version` → primary is `doctor`/`update`/`help`/`completion`/`version` → `--json`/`--json=…` (anywhere in argv before `--`) → global `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` (`--non-interactive`/`--yes`/`--no-input`/`--force`) → command-scoped no-prompt opts in `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` (currently `channels remove --delete`, which is documented as no-prompt) → sidecar dir empty → fresh decline marker. All flag checks honor the `--` argv terminator.

The platform check is hoisted to the call site in `runCli` so non-darwin pays zero dynamic-import cost.

## Headless one-shot warning

Added in `legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts`. Fires when `loadLegacyOAuthSidecarMaterial` returns null on darwin with `allowKeychainPrompt: false` (i.e., env/file seeds were tried and Keychain was blocked). Rate-limited to one warn per process so embedded turns log it once per gateway lifetime, not per attempt. Guarded by the same VITEST env signals as the keychain read so unrelated test runs don't surface it.

## Regression tests

- `src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — table-driven preflight skip checks for each rule (including the new command-scoped `channels remove --delete` skip with positive cases, anti-regression for `channels remove` without `--delete`, `--delete` outside the `channels remove` path, and `--delete` after a `--` terminator), plus end-to-end migrate / decline / permanent skip using `createOpenClawTestState`.
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — exercises the new headless warn path: emit once when blocked on darwin with no env/file seeds; never emit on non-darwin.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/commands/agents.add.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` — 80/80 green.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` clean on all touched files; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- Wider `src/agents/auth-profiles` shard has the same 5 file / 26 test pre-existing flakes vs `origin/main` (`--isolate=false` cross-file mock leakage in unrelated OAuth tests). My new tests do not contribute to those failures; if anything, isolating mine reduces the count.

## Scope notes

- Did not touch `agents.add.test.ts` / `stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` test descriptions that still contain `sidecar-backed`; those are dev-facing and reference implementation, not UI copy.
- Did not externalize `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR` to docs anywhere except `docs/gateway/doctor.md` accordion 5 since it is an escape hatch, not a normal-user knob.
- No changes to `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` itself beyond the wording rename — the migration semantics are intentionally identical between the doctor-driven and CLI-startup-driven paths.
- `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` is a separate data-driven list from `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` deliberately: `--delete` only means "no prompt" on `channels remove`, so it must not be promoted to the global skip set where it would over-match unrelated commands.

## Out of scope

- Removing the legacy sidecar code paths entirely. Tracked separately and should wait for the long tail to migrate.
- Changes to `openclaw doctor --fix` behavior itself; the migration path it runs is already correct.

## Related

- Closes #85083
- Companion to #85074 (file/env-seed bucket) and #85028 (fail-fast on missing refresh token)
- Original surface: #84893 / #84752 / #83312

target.title: telegram: allow last blockquote to be expandable
target.body:
## Summary - Adds Telegram formatter support for Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Preserves existing normal blockquote rendering. - Adds formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. ## Changes - Render expandable blockquote metadata as `
`. - Preserve existing blockquote rendering behavior. - Add formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. - Problem: OpenClaw's Telegram formatter could not represent Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Solution: Add explicit formatter support for expandable blockquotes. - What changed: Telegram blockquote rendering and related formatter tests. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Gateway orchestration, runtime delivery, auth, model routing, and default channel behavior. ## Motivation Telegram supports expandable blockquotes natively. OpenClaw's Telegram formatter should be able to emit that supported HTML form when markdown/IR metadata requests it. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #84082 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Coordination with #84082 Related PR #84082 handles the raw Telegram HTML sanitizer path for user-supplied `
` markup. This PR handles the generated formatter path: markdown/formatter input can opt in with `expandFinalBlockquote: true` and emit Telegram-native `
` output. These paths are complementary and can land separately. This PR does not depend on #84082 because it generates trusted Telegram HTML after markdown formatting rather than preserving a user-supplied raw HTML attribute through sanitizer cleanup. If maintainers want both raw-HTML passthrough and generated formatter support, #84082 and this PR should remain aligned on the same Telegram-native output form. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram expandable blockquote formatting was verified in a real OpenClaw setup using the Telegram integration. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the installed-runtime formatter command below, then observed the rendered message in Telegram in collapsed and expanded states. - Evidence after fix: Redacted live formatter output plus direct observation in Telegram of the collapsed and expanded states. Public screenshots were removed from the PR body because the original captures included live Telegram identifiers. - Console output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:[redacted]
``` - Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the trailing blockquote collapsed initially and expanded it in-place when tapped. The formatter emitted Telegram HTML containing `
` when `expandFinalBlockquote: true` was passed. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. Formatter command: ```sh node --input-type=module <<'EOF' const mod = await import('file:///opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/format-AZK6G0is.js'); const sample = [ 'DM expandable signature test', '', '> Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01', '> GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification', '> Grok: none', '> Ollama-14B: none', '> session agent:main:telegram:direct:[redacted]' ].join('\n'); console.log(mod.a(sample, { textMode: 'markdown', expandFinalBlockquote: true })); EOF ``` Formatter output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:[redacted]
``` ## Verification - Ran the Telegram formatter tests. - Verified live Telegram rendering in a real OpenClaw installation. - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram formatter can now emit native expandable blockquote HTML. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live installed-runtime formatter check above, then verified the rendered result in Telegram with a private local collapsed/expanded check. Public proof in this PR body is redacted. - Evidence after fix: See the Evidence section below for the redacted live formatter command output and the note about the private Telegram check. - Observed result after fix: Telegram displays the blockquote collapsed initially and expands it when tapped. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. - Before evidence: Formatter lacked an expandable blockquote output path. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Telegram formatter only emitted normal blockquote HTML and did not carry expandable blockquote metadata through to Telegram's native `
` form. - Missing detection / guardrail: Formatter coverage did not include expandable blockquote rendering. - Contributing context (if known): N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: Telegram formatter tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: Expandable blockquote metadata renders as `
`, while normal blockquotes continue rendering normally. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is a deterministic formatter output decision. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing blockquote formatter coverage covered normal blockquotes. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes OpenClaw can now render Telegram expandable blockquotes when requested by the formatter input. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw install - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel enabled ### Steps 1. Render Telegram markdown/IR containing expandable blockquote metadata. 2. Send or observe the rendered output in Telegram. 3. Expand the blockquote in Telegram. ### Expected Telegram receives `
` for expandable blockquote input and displays it as an expandable blockquote. ### Actual Telegram displays the blockquote as expandable, while normal blockquote rendering remains unchanged. ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) The original Telegram screenshots were removed from the public PR body because they showed a live direct/session identifier. The visible Telegram check was still performed locally; maintainers can request a fresh redacted or maintainer-controlled capture if they want media proof before merge. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Formatter test coverage and live Telegram expandable blockquote rendering. - Edge cases checked: Normal blockquote rendering remains normal. - What you did **not** verify: Rendering across every Telegram client/platform. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Telegram HTML support may vary slightly across Telegram clients. - Mitigation: The change uses Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form and keeps existing normal blockquote behavior unchanged.

target.title: fix(install): verify node binary after auto-install and suppress ioctl errors
target.body:
## Problem

The Node.js auto-installer reports "Node.js v22 installed" without verifying the binary is actually available on PATH. On WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04, the NodeSource setup script produces ioctl errors (interactive terminal prompts in a non-interactive context), apt may fail silently, but the installer prints a success message and then immediately crashes with "Node.js v22+ is required."

Two root causes:
1. The NodeSource setup script (`bash "$tmp"`) runs without `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`, so it attempts interactive terminal operations that produce ioctl errors on WSL2
2. The installer prints `ui_success "Node.js v24 installed"` unconditionally after the apt call, without verifying `node` is on PATH

## Fix

1. Pass `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` to the NodeSource setup script execution (both root and sudo paths)
2. Run `activate_supported_node_on_path` and verify the active node meets the minimum version requirement (`node_is_at_least_required`) before printing the success message. If node is missing or too old, report the actual failure and exit instead of falsely reporting success.

Fixes #73837

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Node.js auto-installer now (1) passes `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` via `sudo -E env` to suppress ioctl errors on WSL2/Linux, and (2) verifies the active node binary meets the minimum version after package installation before reporting success. A failed or incomplete install now exits with a clear error instead of falsely reporting "Node.js installed."

**Real environment tested:** Clean Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS Docker container (no Node.js preinstalled), and macOS 15.5 (Darwin 25.5.0), Node v26.0.0, bash 5.3.9, openclaw dev checkout at `a65f332744`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1. Ran the patched `node_is_at_least_required` guard on a **clean Ubuntu 24.04 container with no Node.js installed**, sourcing the functions directly from the patched `scripts/install.sh`:

```console
$ docker run --rm -v ./scripts/install.sh:/tmp/install.sh:ro ubuntu:24.04 bash -c '
  echo "Container: $(cat /etc/os-release | grep PRETTY_NAME | cut -d= -f2)"
  echo "Node.js: $(which node 2>/dev/null || echo "NOT INSTALLED")"
  source <(sed -n "/^NODE_MIN_MAJOR=/,/^NODE_MIN_VERSION=/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  source <(sed -n "/^parse_node_version_components_for_binary()/,/^}/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  source <(sed -n "/^parse_node_version_components()/,/^}/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  source <(sed -n "/^node_is_at_least_required()/,/^}/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  echo "Minimum required: v${NODE_MIN_VERSION}"
  echo ""
  echo "--- No node (clean container) ---"
  node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS: rejected"
  mkdir -p /tmp/fn
  echo "--- v18.0.0 (old LTS) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v18.0.0" > /tmp/fn/node; chmod +x /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS: rejected v18.0.0"
  echo "--- v22.18.0 (one minor below minimum) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v22.18.0" > /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS: rejected v22.18.0"
  echo "--- v22.19.0 (exactly minimum) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v22.19.0" > /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: accepted v22.19.0" || echo "FAIL"
  echo "--- v24.15.0 (recommended) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v24.15.0" > /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: accepted v24.15.0" || echo "FAIL"
'
Container: "Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS"
Node.js: NOT INSTALLED
Minimum required: v22.19

--- No node (clean container) ---
PASS: rejected
--- v18.0.0 (old LTS) ---
PASS: rejected v18.0.0
--- v22.18.0 (one minor below minimum) ---
PASS: rejected v22.18.0
--- v22.19.0 (exactly minimum) ---
PASS: accepted v22.19.0
--- v24.15.0 (recommended) ---
PASS: accepted v24.15.0
```

Step 2. Verified `DEBIAN_FRONTEND` propagation in both root and sudo NodeSource setup paths:

```console
$ grep -n "DEBIAN_FRONTEND.*bash.*tmp\|sudo.*DEBIAN_FRONTEND.*bash" scripts/install.sh
1643:                run_quiet_step "Configuring NodeSource repository" env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"
1646:                run_quiet_step "Configuring NodeSource repository" sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"
```

Step 3. Verified post-install guard ordering (guard runs BEFORE success message):

```console
$ awk '/node_is_at_least_required/{print NR": "$0}' scripts/install.sh | head -5
1340: node_is_at_least_required() {
1523:     if node_is_at_least_required; then
1581:         if node_is_at_least_required; then
1678:         if ! node_is_at_least_required; then
$ awk '/ui_success.*Node.js.*installed/{print NR": "$0}' scripts/install.sh
1611:         ui_success "Node.js installed"
1632:             ui_success "Node.js v${NODE_DEFAULT_MAJOR} installed"
1685:         ui_success "Node.js v${NODE_DEFAULT_MAJOR} installed"
```

The `node_is_at_least_required` guard at line 1678 runs before the success message at line 1685, so a failed or incomplete apt install now exits with a clear error instead of falsely reporting success.

**Evidence after fix:** On a clean Ubuntu 24.04 container with no Node preinstalled, the patched guard correctly rejects missing node, old node (v18.0.0), boundary-below node (v22.18.0), and correctly accepts exact-minimum (v22.19.0) and recommended (v24.15.0). The `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` flag is confirmed in both root and sudo apt paths. Guard ordering shows verification runs before the success message.

**Observed result after fix:** All 5 guard scenarios produce correct accept/reject decisions on clean Ubuntu 24.04. The root path uses `env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"` and the sudo path uses `sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"`, preserving the caller environment while suppressing interactive prompts.

**What was not tested:** Full end-to-end `curl | bash` installer run that triggers the actual `apt-get install nodejs` call from NodeSource. The guard functions and env var propagation are verified on real Linux (Ubuntu 24.04 Docker) and macOS. The actual apt package download was not exercised because it requires NodeSource repository credentials and network access that vary by environment.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `d2956fb913` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `d2956fb913`. Focused local validation after the latest stack hardening ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm format:docs:check` - `pnpm lint:docs` - `pnpm docs:check-mdx` - `pnpm docs:check-links` - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` skipped locally because this worktree had no merge-base context; GitHub Actions remains the broad docs authority. - `git diff --check` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

target.body:
## Summary

## Linked context

Requested by @Takhoffman in the source issue.

## Real behavior proof

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm check:changed
- Evidence after fix: command output from pnpm check:changed in CI or local executor log.

- What was not tested: runtime OpenClaw behavior, because this change only updates repository policy text.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this worker was read-only and emitted the implementation artifact; the executor should apply the patch and capture command output.

This PR adds a bundled `channel-broker` plugin, a public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol, constrained-provider capability metadata for WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/BlueBubbles-style providers, docs, tests, and related plugin/test/labeler plumbing.

PR surface: Source +2316, Tests +2292, Docs +424, Config +78, Generated 0, Other +29. Total +5139 across 56 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blocker: source inspection shows the runtime descriptor requests `setChannelBrokerRuntime` from `runtime-api.ts`, while that file does not export it and the loader throws on missing named exports. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can fix the missing runtime setter export and add a guardrail; final merge still needs human approval for the new public SDK and signed webhook boundary.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 4 - Constrained/device-bound provider capabilities - Role: Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. - Head: `9028d38713` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `9028d38713`. Focused local validation after the latest stack hardening ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm format:docs:check` - `pnpm lint:docs` - `pnpm docs:check-mdx` - `pnpm docs:check-links` - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` skipped locally because this worktree had no merge-base context; GitHub Actions remains the broad docs authority. - `git diff --check` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds Phase 4 constrained/device-bound provider metadata to the Channel Broker contract. - Covers WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles-backed iMessage bridges as broker provider capabilities, not native plugin rewrites. - Stacks on Phase 3 official/app capability matrix (#86164), Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86114 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86148, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds `constraints` and `badges` to broker platform/provider capabilities. - Models constrained provider facts such as: - `businessApi`, `cloudApi`, `providerHosted` - `deviceBound`, `linkedDevice`, `qrPairing`, `sessionFragile` - `selfHosted`, `phoneNumberRequired`, `signalCli` - `macHostRequired`, `messagesSignedIn`, `privateApiOptional`, `privateApiRequired` - `externalBridge` - Exposes resolved provider capabilities in channel-broker status snapshots so UI/status can show constrained-provider badges. - Adds WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage target proof through `channel-broker`. - Documents constrained provider expectations in Channel Broker, SDK, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles migration docs. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Constrained providers expose hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed limits through broker `constraints`/`badges`, and WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage still route through provider-owned broker requests. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that evaluated constrained-provider capability support and called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` for `broker:whatsapp:direct:15551234567`, `broker:signal:direct:+15551234567`, and `broker:imessage:group:chat-guid-1?threadId=imessage-thread-1`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "constrainedProviders": { "whatsappBusiness": true, "whatsappDeviceBound": false, "signalDeviceBound": true }, "outbound": [ { "label": "whatsapp-direct", "platform": "whatsapp", "conversation": { "id": "15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "signal-direct", "platform": "signal", "conversation": { "id": "+15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "imessage-group", "platform": "imessage", "conversation": { "id": "chat-guid-1", "type": "group", "threadId": "imessage-thread-1" } } ] } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path distinguished WhatsApp Business-style support from device-bound WhatsApp requirements, accepted Signal's self-hosted/device-bound constraint set, and produced broker provider requests for WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage. - **What was not tested:** Live WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, or BlueBubbles-backed provider traffic; this PR proves the broker contract/schema/docs layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/status.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not implement Phase 5 legacy-plugin freeze. - Next phase: #86115 freezes new legacy native-channel feature work after broker migration paths exist. target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the regression shield. It turns the recurring channel pain into shared conformance: signed inbound webhooks, normalization, dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. sessions, routing, allowlists

receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport

Current head: `b271b61316`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2A Channel Broker conformance baseline on top of #86096. - Extends `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` with inbound normalization, dedupe-key, provider capability normalization, and platform capability checks. - Adds bundled broker conformance coverage for durable inbound receive, durable final send receipts, retryable receipt failures, preview/progress capability gates, and `/verbose`/tool-message `channelData`. ## Rollout Context Closes #86109 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86133, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. This PR intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack routing. Native defaults stay unchanged until the Phase 2B/2C/2D proofs land behind opt-in broker routing. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** The broker conformance baseline normalizes inbound events, builds durable dedupe keys, evaluates preview/progress capabilities, and sends outbound requests through the bundled broker lifecycle without platform-specific core branches. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that imported `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts`, created a Telegram inbound event, built the durable dedupe key, evaluated Discord preview/progress capabilities, and sent broker outbound requests through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "inboundDedupeKey": "acme:bot-main:telegram:live-proof-telegram-1", "previewProgressSupported": true } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path produced the expected durable inbound key and capability decision outside the Vitest harness, proving the Phase 2A contract surface can carry the normalized lifecycle facts that later channel migrations consume. - **What was not tested:** Live provider network traffic and the full local suite; those remain covered by later provider proofs and GitHub Actions. ### Behavior The broker contract now has a conformance harness for the lifecycle paths that regress most often across maintained chat channels: inbound normalization/dedupe, durable final sends, provider receipts, retry/failure propagation, preview/progress capability checks, and `/verbose` tool-message metadata. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-conformance` - Head: `b271b61316` (current Phase 2A head) ### Steps 1. Normalized a synthetic Telegram inbound event through the SDK and accepted it through the durable inbound receive journal. 2. Replayed the same dedupe key while pending and after completion to prove duplicate/redelivery handling. 3. Sent a synthetic Slack final message through the bundled broker runtime and verified provider receipt commit material. 4. Returned a retryable Discord provider receipt and verified the send fails with structured receipt context. 5. Built preview/finalize broker requests and asserted Discord preview/progress support through declared capabilities. 6. Passed `/verbose` tool-message policy through broker `channelData` without platform-specific branching. ### Evidence - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - 1 file passed, 7 tests passed. - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 3 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result The SDK helpers normalize provider payloads without dropping native metadata, the bundled plugin refuses non-sent provider receipts as durable-send failures, and conformance tests prove the current broker path can represent the Phase 2 lifecycle requirements without changing user-visible channel defaults. ### Not Tested - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. - Live Telegram, Discord, or Slack provider traffic. Those are intentionally scoped to Phase 2B/2C/2D. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

target.title: fix(installer): avoid before with npm release-age configs
target.body:
Fixes #84743

## Summary
- detect raw npmrc `min-release-age` settings in the Unix hosted installer scripts before considering npm's computed `before` value
- keep the freshness bypass on `--min-release-age=0` when raw config already contains `min-release-age`, avoiding npm's mutually exclusive `--before` combination
- add shell-level regression coverage for both `install.sh` and `install-cli.sh`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Unix installer no longer emits `--before=<timestamp>` when the user's raw npmrc contains `min-release-age`, even if `npm config get before` would return a computed cutoff.

**Real environment tested:** Local macOS shell environment in `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-84743`, using the real OpenClaw `scripts/install.sh` function and real npm against a temporary npmrc plus temporary npm global prefix. The live proof installed a temporary local package into the temporary prefix so the machine's global npm install location was not mutated.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a temporary npmrc containing `min-release-age=7`, a temporary npm global prefix, and a tiny local package with a bin entry. Ran real npm with the conflicting old-style command shape: `npm --userconfig <temp-npmrc> --before=2026-05-21T00:20:45.000Z config get before`. Then sourced patched OpenClaw `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=<temp-npmrc>`, and `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<temp-prefix>`, and ran `run_npm_global_install <temp-package> <temp-log>`. Supplemental checks: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts`; `bash -n scripts/install.sh scripts/install-cli.sh`; `git diff --check`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output from the real npm/OpenClaw installer proof:

```text
npm_conflict_status=1
npm_conflict_first_line=Exit prior to config file resolving
installer_status=0
last_npm_install_cmd=env -u NPM_CONFIG_BEFORE -u npm_config_before -u NPM_CONFIG_MIN_RELEASE_AGE -u npm_config_min_release_age -u npm_config_min-release-age SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1 npm --loglevel error --silent --no-fund --no-audit --min-release-age=0 install -g /var/folders/sl/5dkd3zq12dv65j6jx57zq1hc0000gn/T/openclaw-84743-proof-ChuZVt/pkg
installed_bin_exists=yes
install_log_tail=
```

Supplemental regression evidence: Vitest result was 4 test files passed, 59 tests passed. Shell syntax check and `git diff --check` completed with no output.

**Observed result after fix:** For raw npmrc release-age policies, the real `install.sh` path preserved the non-conflicting `--min-release-age=0` bypass instead of switching to `--before=<timestamp>`. npm accepted the install command and installed the temporary package into the temporary prefix, while the old conflicting `--before` command shape reproduced npm's `Exit prior to config file resolving` failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not run the hosted `curl | bash` installer or install the published `openclaw` package globally because that would be slower and more invasive than the temporary-prefix proof. I did not change PowerShell installer behavior; the existing `install-ps1` tests were included in the acceptance run.

## Attribution
If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution for Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
